pull_request.title: Bump core Jupyter packages (ipykernel → 7.1.0; ipython → 9.9.0; jupyter-core → 5.9.1) and upgrade numpy, pandas, transformers, datasets, accelerate
pull_request.body:
<!--- Provide a general summary of your changes in the Title above -->

## Description
<!--- Describe your changes in detail -->

## How Has This Been Tested?
<!--- Please describe in detail how you tested your changes. -->
<!--- Include details of your testing environment, and the tests you ran to -->
<!--- see how your change affects other areas of the code, etc. -->

Self checklist (all need to be checked):
- [ ] Ensure that you have run `make test` (`gmake` on macOS) before asking for review
- [ ] Changes to everything except `Dockerfile.konflux` files should be done in `odh/notebooks` and automatically synced to `rhds/notebooks`. For Konflux-specific changes, modify `Dockerfile.konflux` files directly in `rhds/notebooks` as these require special attention in the downstream repository and flow to the upcoming RHOAI release.

## Merge criteria:
<!--- This PR will be merged by any repository approver when it meets all the points in the checklist -->
<!--- Go over all the following points, and put an `x` in all the boxes that apply. -->

- [ ] The commits are squashed in a cohesive manner and have meaningful messages.
- [ ] Testing instructions have been added in the PR body (for PRs involving changes that are not immediately obvious).
- [ ] The developer has manually tested the changes and verified that the changes work


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
  * Bumped core Jupyter packages (ipykernel → 7.1.0; ipython → 9.9.0; jupyter-core → 5.9.1) and upgraded numpy, pandas, transformers, datasets, accelerate.
  * Added/updated runtime deps (nbconvert, nbformat, papermill, pyzmq, prompt-toolkit, requests, tornado, traitlets, urllib3).
  * Improved install/build steps for specific architectures with targeted pyzmq handling and OpenSSL/build flag adjustments.

* **Tests**
  * Improved test utilities for directory recognition and version-consistency checks; expanded exact package pinning in tests.

<sub>✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.</sub>
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

@@ -614,10 +614,10 @@ wheels = [{ url = "https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/0e/61/66938bbb5fc52db

-version = "6.30.1"
+version = "7.1.0"
 marker = "implementation_name == 'cpython' and sys_platform == 'linux'"
-sdist = { url = "https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/bb/76/11082e338e0daadc89c8ff866185de11daf67d181901038f9e139d109761/ipykernel-6.30.1.tar.gz", upload-time = 2025-08-04T15:47:35Z, size = 166260, hashes = { sha256 = "6abb270161896402e76b91394fcdce5d1be5d45f456671e5080572f8505be39b" } }
-wheels = [{ url = "https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/fc/c7/b445faca8deb954fe536abebff4ece5b097b923de482b26e78448c89d1dd/ipykernel-6.30.1-py3-none-any.whl", upload-time = 2025-08-04T15:47:32Z, size = 117484, hashes = { sha256 = "aa6b9fb93dca949069d8b85b6c79b2518e32ac583ae9c7d37c51d119e18b3fb4" } }]
+sdist = { url = "https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/b9/a4/4948be6eb88628505b83a1f2f40d90254cab66abf2043b3c40fa07dfce0f/ipykernel-7.1.0.tar.gz", upload-time = 2025-10-27T09:46:39Z, size = 174579, hashes = { sha256 = "58a3fc88533d5930c3546dc7eac66c6d288acde4f801e2001e65edc5dc9cf0db" } }
+wheels = [{ url = "https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/a3/17/20c2552266728ceba271967b87919664ecc0e33efca29c3efc6baf88c5f9/ipykernel-7.1.0-py3-none-any.whl", upload-time = 2025-10-27T09:46:37Z, size = 117968, hashes = { sha256 = "763b5ec6c5b7776f6a8d7ce09b267693b4e5ce75cb50ae696aaefb3c85e1ea4c" } }]
 
 [[packages]]
 name = "ipython"

@@ -5,12 +5,12 @@ requires-python = "==3.12.*"
 
 # This is a comprehensive list of python dependencies that Elyra requires to execute Jupyter notebooks.
 dependencies = [
-    "ipykernel~=6.30.1",
-    "ipython~=9.6.0",
+    "ipykernel~=7.1.0",
+    "ipython~=9.9.0",
     "ipython-genutils~=0.2.0",
     "jinja2~=3.1.6",
     "jupyter-client>=8.6.3",
-    "jupyter-core~=5.8.1",
+    "jupyter-core~=5.9.1",
     "markupsafe~=3.0.3",
     "minio~=7.2.18",
     "nbclient~=0.10.2",

@@ -432,6 +432,19 @@ echo "Installing software and packages"
 if [ "$TARGETARCH" = "ppc64le" ] || [ "$TARGETARCH" = "s390x" ]; then
     # We need special flags and environment variables when building packages
     # aipcc does not have some dependencies to build cryptography==43.0.3 on ppc64le, so we need to use pypi.org/simple
+
+    # first, install pyzmq with our extra flags for libzmq
+    # https://github.com/opendatahub-io/notebooks/issues/1172
+    PYZMQ_REQ=$(python3 -c "import tomllib; print(next(f'{p[\"name\"]}=={p[\"version\"]}' for p in tomllib.load(open('pylock.toml', 'rb'))['packages'] if p['name'] == 'pyzmq'))")
+    GRPC_PYTHON_BUILD_SYSTEM_OPENSSL=1 \
+    CFLAGS="${CFLAGS:-} -O3" CXXFLAGS="${CXXFLAGS:-} -O3" \
+    uv pip install --strict --no-deps --no-cache --no-config --no-progress \
+        --verify-hashes --compile-bytecode --index-strategy=unsafe-best-match \
+        --extra-index-url https://pypi.org/simple \
+        "${PYZMQ_REQ}"
+
+    # next, install everything else
+    # this is because matplotlib depends on pybind11 which won't build if we define `undefined`
     GRPC_PYTHON_BUILD_SYSTEM_OPENSSL=1 \
     CFLAGS="-O3" CXXFLAGS="-O3" \
     uv pip install --strict --no-deps --no-cache --no-config --no-progress \

@@ -24,19 +24,19 @@ dependencies = [
     # More information available at:
     #   - https://pypi.org/project/trustyai/
     #   - https://github.com/trustyai-explainability/trustyai-explainability-python/blob/main/requirements.txt
-    "transformers~=4.56.1",
-    "datasets~=4.0.0",
-    "accelerate~=1.10.1; platform_machine != 'ppc64le' and platform_machine != 's390x'",
-    "trustyai~=0.6.2",
+    "transformers~=4.57.3",
+    "datasets~=4.4.1",
+    "accelerate~=1.12.0; platform_machine != 'ppc64le' and platform_machine != 's390x'",
+    "trustyai~=0.6.3",
     # Datascience and useful extensions
     "boto3~=1.40.52",
     "kafka-python-ng~=2.2.3",
     "kfp~=2.14.6",
-    "matplotlib~=3.10.7", # Should be pinned down to this version in order to be compatible with trustyai
-    "numpy~=1.26.4", # Should be pinned down to this version in order to be compatible with trustyai
-    "pandas~=1.5.3", # Should be pinned down to this version in order to be compatible with trustyai
+    "matplotlib~=3.10.7", # Should be pinned down to this version in order to be compatible with trustyai < 0.6.3
+    "numpy~=2.3.5", # Should be pinned down to this version in order to be compatible with trustyai < 0.6.3
+    "pandas~=2.3.3",
     "plotly~=6.3.1",
-    "scikit-learn~=1.7.2", # Should be pinned down to this version in order to be compatible with trustyai
+    "scikit-learn~=1.7.2", # Should be pinned down to this version in order to be compatible with trustyai < 0.6.3
     "scipy~=1.16.2",
     "skl2onnx~=1.19.1",
     "onnxconverter-common~=1.13.0", # Required for skl2onnx, as upgraded version is not compatible with protobuf
@@ -54,7 +54,7 @@ dependencies = [
     "odh-jupyter-trash-cleanup==0.1.1",
 
     "jupyterlab==4.5.2",
-    "jupyter-bokeh~=3.0.5", # trustyai 0.6.1 depends on jupyter-bokeh~=3.0.5
+    "jupyter-bokeh~=4.0.5",
     "jupyter-server~=2.17.0",
     "jupyter-server-proxy~=4.4.0",
     "jupyter-server-terminals~=0.5.3",

@@ -444,6 +444,16 @@ if [ "$TARGETARCH" = "ppc64le" ]; then
     uv pip install --strict --no-deps --no-cache --no-config --no-progress --verify-hashes --compile-bytecode --index-strategy=unsafe-best-match --requirements=./pylock.toml
 elif [ "$TARGETARCH" = "s390x" ]; then
     # For s390x, we need special flags and environment variables for building packages
+
+    # first, install pyzmq with our extra flags for libzmq
+    # https://github.com/opendatahub-io/notebooks/issues/1172
+    PYZMQ_REQ=$(python3 -c "import tomllib; print(next(f'{p[\"name\"]}=={p[\"version\"]}' for p in tomllib.load(open('pylock.toml', 'rb'))['packages'] if p['name'] == 'pyzmq'))")
+    GRPC_PYTHON_BUILD_SYSTEM_OPENSSL=1 \
+    CFLAGS="${CFLAGS:-} -O3" CXXFLAGS="${CXXFLAGS:-} -O3" \
+    uv pip install --strict --no-deps --no-cache --no-config --no-progress --verify-hashes --compile-bytecode --index-strategy=unsafe-best-match "${PYZMQ_REQ}"
+
+    # next, install everything else
+    # this is because matplotlib depends on pybind11 which won't build if we define `undefined`
     GRPC_PYTHON_BUILD_SYSTEM_OPENSSL=1 \
     CFLAGS="-O3" CXXFLAGS="-O3" \
     uv pip install --strict --no-deps --no-cache --no-config --no-progress --verify-hashes --compile-bytecode --index-strategy=unsafe-best-match --requirements=./pylock.toml

@@ -469,6 +463,19 @@ def _skip_unimplemented_manifests(directory: pathlib.Path, call_skip=True) -> bo
     return False
 
 
+def is_image_directory(directory: pathlib.Path) -> bool:
+    """image directory e.g. "ubi9-python-3.11"""
+    try:
+        _ubi, _lang, _python = directory.name.split("-")
+        return True
+    except ValueError:
+        return False
+
+
+def is_dependencies_directory(file: pathlib.Path) -> bool:
+    return "dependencies" in file.relative_to(PROJECT_ROOT).parts
+
+
 @dataclasses.dataclass
 class Manifest:
     filename: pathlib.Path

commit_summaries.message:
fix(tests): include `dependencies/` pyproject.toml files in version alignment test (#2861)

The test_image_pyprojects_version_alignment was only checking
pyproject.toml files in directories matching the pattern
{ubi}-{lang}-{python} (e.g., ubi9-python-3.12), missing files
in the dependencies/ directory.

This caused version mismatches like ipykernel ~=7.1.0 vs ~=6.30.1
to go undetected.

Also, refactor the directory pattern check into helper functions
is_image_directory() and is_dependencies_directory().

RHAIENG-2857: chore(deps): upgrade dependency versions for Elyra deps and in Jupyter TrustyAI notebook (#2861)

The motivating ticket is https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHAIENG-1727, but to upgrade minimal runtime and not introduce multiple package versions across various images to be updated, I had to also update packages in TrustyAI workbench, and that required upgrading TrustyAI.

chore(datascience/Dockerfile): workaround pyzmq build issues on ppc64le and s390x (#2861)

The build on s390x was failing because `CFLAGS="-O3"` was overwriting
the existing CFLAGS (which includes ``-Dundefined=64` needed for QEMU
to handle `ZMQ_CACHELINE_SIZE` detection).

We can't define this globally because that would break matplotlib's pybind11.

Therefore, install pyzmq separately with custom flags and constraints to address
build issues without affecting pybind11 dependencies on these architectures.

TODO: Make equivalent changes in Dockerfile.konflux.cpu in
red-hat-data-services/notebooks fork.
